30 March 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • US Troop Levels in Middle East Increase by 10,000, Surpassing 50,000 Total — Reports Sputnik 30 Mar 2026 -- The number of American troops in the region has surpassed 50,000 — roughly 10,000 more than usual — following the arrival of 2,500 Marines and 2,500 sailors, The New York Times reported.

  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 30 Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, March 30, 2027, the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) and the Iranian Army conducted multiple operations as part of Operation True Promise 4, which was launched immediately after the US-Israeli coalition carried out an unprovoked act of aggression against the Islamic Republic of Iran on February 28.
  • IRGC targets hideout of US Fifth Fleet commanders in Bahrain Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has targeted the hideout of commanders of the US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain as part of a fresh wave of Operation True Promise 4.
  • Iran's Army strikes key Israeli military procurement companies Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian Army says it has used unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to target key military procurement infrastructure in the Israeli-occupied territories.
  • US E-3 reportedly destroyed in Iranian strike; Chinese expert dissects Iran's intelligence, precision targeting of time-sensitive assets Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- Iran has destroyed a US E-3 Sentry stationed at a Saudi air base, according to multiple media reports. So far, Washington has made no public claim on the incident. A Chinese expert told the Global Times on Monday that the strike reflects how Iran orchestrates its intelligence-to-strike chain, from real-time surveillance to the rapid engagement of time-sensitive assets. The expert noted that the precision and destructive effect suggest advances in weapon guidance, while the timing of the attack shows how Tehran has maintained operational tempo despite weeks of airstrikes.
  • IRGC hits US-Israeli pilot hideouts, drone hangers in 87th wave of reprisal attacks Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says it has hit American-Israeli command centers, drone hangars, and pilot hideouts in new missile strikes on their military installations in the region.
  • Iran Army drones strike US radar stations, site for troops deployment in UAE Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army says it has targeted the US radar stations and a site for the deployment of American military forces in the United Arab Emirates with invading drones, amid an ongoing unprovoked war of aggression by a US-Israeli military coalition against the Islamic Republic.
  • IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri martyred after sustaining injuries in US-Israeli aggression Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced the martyrdom of Navy Commander Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri following injuries he sustained in the US-Israeli aggression.

  • NATO intercepts ballistic munition launched from Iran entering Turkish airspace Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 30 Mar 2026 -- A ballistic munition launched from Iran and entering Turkish airspace was neutralized by NATO air and missile defense assets deployed in the Eastern Mediterranean, Türkiye's Defense Ministry said on Monday.

  • Trump Renews Warning To Iran: Reach Deal Soon Or Face Energy Sector Attacks RFE/RL 30 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has renewed his warning to Tehran to reach a deal to end the war soon and open the Strait of Hormuz or he will order air strikes with the aim of "completely obliterating" Iran's oil export hub of Kharg Island, oil wells, and power plants.
  • Trump threatens to invade Iran's Kharg Island 'to take the oil' despite Iran's warnings Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has threatened to invade Kharg Island in order to take control of Iran's oil exports.
  • US President renews threat to Iran amid contradicting claims on 15-point list; observers say Iran war enters a critical phase of warfare and diplomatic maneuvering Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- The Chinese side has stressed on multiple occasions that a drawn-out conflict serves no one's interest, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Monday in response to question regarding US President Donald Trump's claim that a deal with Iran on ending the Middle East conflict could be reached soon, while at the same time not ruling out ground operations as well as more strikes, including ones targeting power stations.
  • Rubio Says Strait of Hormuz Will Reopen With Iran's Consent or Through Military Coalition Sputnik 30 Mar 2026 -- The Strait of Hormuz will reopen either with Iran's approval or through a US-backed international military coalition, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Monday.
  • 'My Favorite Thing is to Take the Oil in Iran' - Trump Sputnik 30 Mar 2026 -- "To be honest with you, my favorite thing is to take the oil in Iran but some stupid people back in the US say: 'why are you doing that?' But they're stupid people," US President Donald Trump told the Financial Times.
  • Trump Says He Wants Iran's Oil, But Also Insists Talks Going 'Extremely Well' RFE/RL 30 Mar 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said he wants to "take the oil in Iran" and perhaps seize Kharg Island, while at the same time insisting that Washington is doing "extremely well" in negotiations with Iran and said he is "pretty sure" a peace deal will be reached.
  • Trump says US could take Iran's Kharg Island Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Sunday that he wants to "take the oil in Iran" and could seize Kharg Island, the country's oil export hub.
  • What to know about U.S. shifting targets, new deployments and diplomatic double game as conflict enters Day 30? People's Daily 30 Mar 2026 -- The U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran that began with targeted "decapitation" strikes on Feb. 28 has spiralled into a multi-front regional war with no end in sight.

  • IRGC Quds Force commander: Yemen's resistance forced US aircraft carrier into humiliating retreat Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Quds Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has expressed solidarity and appreciation to the Islamic Resistance of Yemen, hailing its heroic role in confronting criminal America and the child-killing Israeli regime while defending Islamic values and the oppressed people of Gaza.
  • Any 'provocative act' in Strait of Hormuz will further complicate situation: Iran FM Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi of Iran has warned that any act of provocation by the aggressors and their supporters, including at the United Nations Security Council, regarding the Strait of Hormuz will only lead to further complications of the situation.
  • Get used to new regional order, IRGC's Quds commander tells Israelis Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- A top commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says the Israeli regime must come to terms with new realities in West Asia, which include the growing dominance of the resistance forces in the region.
  • IRGC to deal 'more crushing' blows to enemies after Tangsiri's martyrdom: Cmdr. Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian military commander has warned that the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) will deal "more crushing" blows to the United States and the Israeli regime following the martyrdom of the elite force's Navy chief.
  • Iran says no 'direct' talks held with US despite Trump's claims Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The spokesman for Iran's Foreign Ministry says the country has held no "direct" talks with the United States until now, but has received messages through some mediators regarding the US desire for negotiations.
  • Iran will determine end of war imposed by US, Israel: Military Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters of Iran says Tehran is the party that will determine the end of the ongoing war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Leader, president, parliament speaker, judiciary chief honor martyred IRGC Navy commander Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Top Iranian officials have condoled the martyrdom of Rear Admiral Alireza Tangsiri, commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy, praising his role in strengthening Iran's maritime power and resistance.
  • Nearly 140 elements affiliated with US, Israel arrested in southwest Iran Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Authorities in the southwestern Iranian province of Khuzestan have arrested 138 individuals for collaborating with US-Israeli subversive schemes against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran warns accommodations of US, Israeli commanders, officials deemed legitimate targets Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The spokesperson for the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters of Iran has warned that the accommodations of US and Israeli military commanders and authorities would be deemed as legitimate targets in retaliation for the ongoing war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran's Parliament Seriously Considering Withdrawing from NPT - Lawmaker Sputnik 30 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian parliament is seriously considering withdrawing from the Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT), Alaeddin Boroujerdi, a member of the national security and foreign policy commission of the country's parliament, said on Monday.
  • Iran's Parliament Seriously Considering Withdrawing from NPT - Lawmaker Sputnik 30 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian parliament is seriously considering withdrawing from the Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT), Alaeddin Boroujerdi, a member of the national security and foreign policy commission of the country's parliament, said on Monday.
  • Acting Defense Minister: Iran exercising its inalienable right to self-defense Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Acting Minister of Defense Brigadier General Seyyed Majid Ibn Reza has held an important telephone conversation with Turkish Defense Minister Yaşar Güler amid the ongoing US-Israeli war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.

  • Ending war between USA-Israel and Iran must focus on human rights of Iranians: UN experts OHCHR 30 Mar 2026 -- Military strikes by the United States and Israel have dramatically worsened the human rights situation in Iran, with a growing death toll and exacerbating existing economic and environmental pressures for people in the country, UN experts* warned today.

  • Chinese FM responds to Trump's remark on potential deal to end conflict with Iran, while not ruling out ground operations Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- China has repeatedly emphasized that delaying the war is not in the interests of any party. Only by talking can we avoid more casualties and prevent the spread of the war, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Monday in response to US President Donald Trump's claim that he might soon reach an agreement with Iran to end the conflict in the Middle East, but at the same time he did not rule out the possibility of taking ground actions and carrying out more strikes against Iran.

  • Lavrov Discusses With Foreign Ministers of Persian Gulf Countries Escalation in Region Sputnik 30 Mar 2026 -- Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov discussed with the foreign ministers of Persian Gulf countries the escalation in the region, the Russian Foreign Ministry said on Monday.
  • Press release on videoconference between Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov and foreign ministers of the GCC member states and Jordan Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Mar 2026

  • Foreign Minister Participates in Joint Ministerial Meeting among GCC, Russia and Jordan SPA 30 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah participated today via a video conference in a joint ministerial meeting among the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) states, the Russian Federation, and the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, focusing on the repercussions of Iranian attacks in the region
  • HRH the Crown Prince, Qatari Amir Discuss Repercussions of Military Escalation in the Region SPA 30 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, met today in Jeddah with Amir of the State of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani
  • HRH the Crown Prince Holds Meeting with Jordanian King, Qatari Amir SPA 30 Mar 2026 -- A trilateral meeting was held today in Jeddah, bringing together His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, King Abdullah II ibn Al Hussein of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, and Amir of the State of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani
  • HRH the Crown Prince, Jordanian King Discuss Repercussions of Military Escalation in the Middle East SPA 30 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, met today in Jeddah with King Abdullah II ibn Al Hussein of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Two Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in the Past Few Hours SPA 30 Mar 2026
  • Saudi Arabia Condemns Iranian Attacks on Kuwaiti Military Camp and Infrastructure SPA 30 Mar 2026 -- The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia condemns in the strongest terms the heinous Iranian attacks targeting a camp belonging to the Kuwaiti Armed Forces, as well as a power and water desalination plant in the State of Kuwait, which resulted in injuries to a number of Kuwaiti Armed Forces personnel
  • Defense Minister Discusses Regional Developments with British Counterpart SPA 30 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Defense Prince Khalid bin Salman bin Abdulaziz met today in Riyadh with British Secretary of State for Defence John Healey, during which they reviewed the strategic defense partnership between the two friendly nations and explored opportunities for its further development. They also discussed regional developments and their repercussions for the security and stability of both the region and the world. The two sides condemned the ongoing Iranian attacks targeting the Kingdom
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Cruise Missile Directed Towards Eastern Region Detected and Destroyed SPA 30 Mar 2026

  • Turkish Foreign Minister Fidan joins four-nation talks in Islamabad on Middle East tensions Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 30 Mar 2026 -- Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan attended a Türkiye-Egypt-Pakistan-Saudi Arabia foreign ministers' meeting held in Islamabad on Sunday, Anadolu Agency reported.
  • Human rights in Iran: Council extends sanctions regime until April 2027 EC/CEU 30 Mar 2026 -- The Council today decided to extend EU restrictive measures responding to serious human rights violations in Iran until 13 April 2027.
  • HE GCCSG: The Brutal Iranian Targeting of a Camp Belonging to the Kuwaiti Armed Forces, Resulting in the Injury of 10 of its Personnel, is Clear Evidence of its Hostile Intent Toward the State of Kuwait and the GCC Countries Gulf Cooperation Council 30 Mar 2026
  • HE GCCSG: GCC-Jordan-Russia Meeting Discussed Iranian Aggressions Against Council States and Jordan; Reviewed Regional Developments Gulf Cooperation Council 30 Mar 2026
  • Aboul Gheit condemns Iran's "reprehensible" attacks on Kuwait's electricity and water desalination facilities League of Arab States 30 Mar 2026 -- Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, strongly condemned the reprehensible Iranian attacks on electricity and water desalination facilities in the State of Kuwait.
  • Ukraine actively involved in US-Israeli aggression against Iran: Envoy to UN Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- A senior Iranian diplomat condemns Ukraine's admission to the dispatch of "hundreds of experts" to the region to confront Iran, saying Kiev is actively participating in the military aggression launched by the United State and the Israeli regime against the Islamic Republic.
  • Belgium open to deploying minehunters in Strait of Hormuz Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 30 Mar 2026 -- Belgian Foreign Minister Maxime Prevot signaled openness Monday to a possible deployment of the country's minehunters in the Strait of Hormuz once the situation stabilizes, according to Anadolu Agency.
  • It Has Been a Very Fruitful Visit to the Middle East and the Gulf Region: We Have Agreed on Opportunities to Strengthen Air Defense, on the Joint Development of Defense Production, and on Energy Cooperation - Address by the President President of Ukraine 30 Mar 2026
  • Ukraine and the Countries of the Middle East Are Building Mutually Beneficial Partnerships for Years to Come - Results of the President's Visits to Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Qatar, and Jordan President of Ukraine
  • Joint Press Statement at the Conclusion of the Visit of the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Canada to the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia Canada 30 Mar 2026

  • Israeli-US aggression on Iran wiping $120bn from Abu Dhabi, Dubai markets: Report Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- The United Arab Emirates (UAE) is facing its most serious economic shock in decades following the unprovoked aggression against Iran by the United States and Israel, a report says.

  • Iran's daily oil revenue doubles amid US-Israeli aggression: Report Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Iran has reportedly doubled its daily oil revenue since late February, leveraging the ongoing US-Israeli pressure aggression into a strategic financial advantage.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • F-35A Lightning IIs arrive at Misawa Air Base US Air Force 30 Mar 2026 -- F-35A Lightning IIs assigned to the 13th Fighter Squadron arrived at Misawa Air Base, March 28, marking the beginning of the squadron's transition to fifth-generation airpower and the permanent stationing of the joint force's most advanced tactical aircraft in northern Japan.
  • USAF, Army integrate air defense capabilities during Freedom Shield 26 US Air Force 30 Mar 2026 -- The 51st Security Forces Squadron and 35th Air Defense Artillery Brigade conducted joint training during exercise Freedom Shield 26 at Osan Air Base, March 9-13.
  • Navy Commissions Submarine USS Massachusetts (SSN 798) US Navy 30 Mar 2026 -- The Navy commissioned the Virginia-class fast-attack submarine USS Massachusetts (SSN 798) in a traditional ceremony held March 28 at Boston Harbor.
  • Portsmouth Naval Shipyard Undocks USS Albany Ahead of Schedule at Naval Submarine Base New London US Navy 30 Mar 2026 -- Portsmouth Naval Shipyard safely undocked Los Angeles-class attack submarine USS Albany (SSN 753) March 26 at Naval Submarine Base (SUBASE) New London, marking a significant milestone in its maintenance and modernization availability.
  • USS Gerald R. Ford Arrives in Split, Croatia US Navy 30 Mar 2026 -- The world's largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78) anchored in the Port of Split, Croatia, March 28, following an Adriatic Sea transit from Souda Bay, Greece, where the ship conducted a working port to resupply, refuel, and conduct repairs, March 23-26.
  • USS Blue Ridge departs Laem Chabang, Thailand U.S. 7th Fleet 30 Mar 2026 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) and embarked U.S. 7th Fleet departed Laem Chabang, Thailand following a planned port visit March 30, 2026.
  • Interagency forum looks at U.S. investment in Africa to support U.S. security AFRICOM 30 Mar 2026 -- More than 160 civilian and military representatives from the Departments of War, State and Commerce met March 26-27, 2026, at Ramstein Air Base, Germany, to identify strategic priorities and opportunities for the United States in Africa as part of the Africa Strategic Integration Dialogue (ASID).
  • U.S. Strengthens Maritime Security Cooperation with Kenya During NAVAF Commander's Visit AFRICOM 30 Mar 2026 -- U.S. Naval Forces Africa (NAVAF) strengthened its longstanding security partnership with Kenya during Adm. George Wikoff's visit to Nairobi and Mombasa, March 24-25.

  • NATO's Multinational MRTT Fleet (MMF) declares Full Operating Capability (FOC) NATO Support and Procurement Agency (NSPA) 30 Mar 2026 -- On 26 March 2026, the Multinational MultiRole Tanker Transport (MRTT) Unit (MMU) declared Full Operating Capability (FOC) during an event at the Eindhoven Air Base, Netherlands, in the presence of representatives from NATO, EU and the eight participating nations.

  • Statement by the President of Ukraine During a Joint Press Conference with the Prime Minister of Bulgaria President of Ukraine 30 Mar 2026
  • Ukraine and Bulgaria Signed a Security Cooperation Agreement President of Ukraine 30 Mar 2026
  • Security Cooperation and Energy Support: President of Ukraine Met with the Prime Minister of Bulgaria President of Ukraine 30 Mar 2026
  • Private air defense is now operational: first intercepts of enemy air threats confirmed Ukraine MOD 30 Mar 2026 -- The pilot project launched by the Government to involve the private sector in the air defense system is already yielding initial results. One of the companies participating in the project has already formed its own air defense group.
  • Disrupting russian oil exports along the Baltic Sea coast: impact and consequences of strikes in the Leningrad Oblast Ukraine MOD 30 Mar 2026 -- Over the past week, Ukraine's Defence Forces conducted a series of coordinated precision strikes on infrastructure in russia's Leningrad Oblast that support the aggressor's war machine. Strikes on russian ports along the Baltic coast have blocked a significant share of russian oil exports.
    • 30 Mar 2026

  • U.S. senators see results of drone weapon tests in Taiwan CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- A bipartisan delegation of U.S. senators visited Taiwan's National Chung-Shan Institute of Science and Technology (NCSIST) on Monday, where they saw displays showing the results of a live-fire test of a drone-mounted warhead.
  • U.S. agrees to HIMARS payment delay; talks on other systems ongoing: MND CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- The United States has agreed to Taiwan's request to defer the deadline for an initial payment on a key U.S.- made weapons system amid a budget impasse, Defense Minister Wellington Koo (顧立雄) said Monday at a legislative session.
  • Taichung mayor proposes defense budget shift toward drones CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- Taichung Mayor Lu Shiow-yen (盧秀燕) of the opposition Kuomintang (KMT) said Monday that Taiwan's special defense budget should prioritize drones over submarines and range from NT$800 billion (US$25 billion) to NT$1 trillion.
  • Visiting U.S. senators call on Taiwan to pass special defense bill CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- A group of visiting U.S. senators on Monday highlighted the importance of "peace through strength," while calling on Taiwan to swiftly pass a special defense budget bill to fund planned purchases of U.S. weapons systems for self-defense.

  • New Littoral Manoeuvre Group established in Brisbane Australia DOD 30 Mar 2026 -- The Australian Army has recognised the establishment of its new Littoral Manoeuvre Group with a ceremonial parade at Gallipoli Barracks, Brisbane.

  • DELIVERY OF AGRAY - THE FOURTH ANTI-SUBMARINE WARFARE SHALLOW WATER CRAFT BUILT BY GRSE, KOLKATA India PIB 30 Mar 2026 -- 'Agray', the fourth of eight ASW SWC (Anti-Submarine Warfare Shallow Water Craft), indigenously designed and built by Garden Reach Shipbuilders and Engineers (GRSE), Kolkata, has been delivered to the Indian Navy on 30 Mar 2026 at Kolkata.

  • Israel MOD Signs Approx $48M Deal with Elbit Systems for Tens of Thousands of Land Munitions Israel MOD 30 Mar 2026 -- As part of its ongoing effort to bolster IDF operational endurance and readiness for the evolving campaign, the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) today (Monday) announced a deal worth approximately $48 million (over NIS 150 million) to procure tens of thousands of 155mm artillery shells from Elbit Systems.

  • Commission adopts €1.5 billion work programme to boost European and Ukrainian defence industry European Commission 30 Mar 2026 -- The European Commission approved today a €1.5 billion work programme under the European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP) to enhance and modernise Europe's defence industry, boost production capacity and secure technological advancement and resilience.

  • Maritime security: Council updates mandates of EU naval operations ASPIDES and ATALANTA EC/CEU 30 Mar 2026 -- The Council today adopted two decisions amending the mandates of the EU's naval operations EUNAVFOR ASPIDES and EUNAVFOR ATALANTA.

  • EDA to coordinate ammunition qualification tests European Defence Agency 30 Mar 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) will coordinate the Joint Ammunition Qualification (JAQ) programme, funded with €50 million from the European Commission under the European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP). The project aims to streamline how ammunition is tested and approved across countries involved in the programme. It will initially focus on 155 mm artillery ammunition, identified as a priority by Member States.

  • R. Kaunas: NATO Allied commitment stays firm - Croatian presence in Lithuania remains Lithuania MND 30 Mar 2026 -- Minister of National Defence Robertas Kaunas met with Minister of Defence of Croatia Ivan Anušić who arrived in Lithuania to visit with his troops deployed to the NATO Multinational Battle Group in Rukla.

  • Defence Minister's meeting with his counterpart from the Slovak Republic Romania - Ministry of National Defence 30 Mar 2026 -- Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with Robert Kaliňák, his counterpart from the Slovak Republic, on Friday, March 27. The meeting took place in the context of the official visit paid to Romania by the Slovak Prime Minister, at the invitation of the Romanian Prime Minister.
  • Defence Minister Miruță's meeting with the Ambassador of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, at the MoND Headquarters Romania - Ministry of National Defence 30 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, March 30, Minister of National Defence, Radu Miruță, had a meeting with the Ambassador of the Kingdom of the Netherlands to Bucharest, HE Willemijn van Haaften.

  • Sea mines clearance: A new dimension of difficulty UN News 30 Mar 2026 -- Clearing mines laid at sea can be an "extremely challenging and very dangerous" undertaking according to a UN mines expert.

  • Arms Dealer Pleads Guilty to Conspiring to Export American-Made Ammunition Used in War Against Ukraine US Dept. of Justice 30 Mar 2026 -- Today, Italian national Manfred Gruber pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit export control violations. Gruber illegally exported ammunition worth over $540,000 from the United States to Kyrgyzstan, via companies that the defendant and his co-conspirator controlled in Italy. After reaching Kyrgyzstan, most of this ammunition was subsequently reexported to Russia.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio with Hashem Ahelbarra of Al Jazeera US Dept. of State 30 Mar 2026
  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio with George Stephanopoulos of ABC Good Morning America US Dept. of State 30 Mar 2026
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Georgia Prime Minister Kobakhidze US Dept. of State 30 Mar 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 30 March 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 30 Mar 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots | Roaring Lion (Lebanon)

  • Hezbollah targets Israeli naval base in Haifa with precision missiles Press TV 30 Mar 2026 -- Hezbollah announced in a statement that the strike targeted the naval base in the city of Haifa on Monday and achieved the desired objectives.

  • UN condemns killing of two more peacekeepers in Lebanon UN News 30 Mar 2026 -- The United Nations has condemned two consecutive days of deadly attacks on peacekeepers serving with the UN Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL), amid rising hostilities between Israeli forces and Hezbollah militants.
  • The image from Gaza that still haunts me: Palestine relief agency chief UN News 30 Mar 2026 -- Asking the softly spoken, veteran humanitarian worker Philippe Lazzarini how he feels as he comes to the end of his second term as the head of the UN agency for Palestinians, UNRWA, is perhaps an unfair question.
  • UN chief condemns incident killing UNIFIL peacekeeper in southern Lebanon People's Daily 30 Mar 2026 -- UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres on Sunday strongly condemned the incident earlier in the day that resulted in the death of an Indonesian peacekeeper of the United Nations Interim Force in Lebanon (UNIFIL) at his position in southern Lebanon, amid hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah, his spokesperson said.

  • Finland to grant humanitarian assistance to Lebanon Finnish Government 30 Mar 2026 -- Finland will grant two million euros in humanitarian assistance to the UN Refugee Agency UNHCR's operation in Lebanon to support refugees and internally displaced persons.
  • Council of Arab Ministers of Social Affairs convenes extraordinary session League of Arab States 30 Mar 2026 -- Pursuant to the directives of His Excellency Mr. Ahmed Aboul Gheit, Secretary-General of the League of Arab States, the Executive Bureau of the Council of Arab Ministers of Social Affairs convened an extraordinary session on 26 March to examine developments in the social and humanitarian conditions in the State of Palestine and the Lebanese Republic, in light of the escalating situation and the inhumane practices of Israel, the occupying Power.
  • The OIC Condemns Israel - the Occupying Power - for Preventing the Latin Patriarch from Entering the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in Occupied Al-Quds Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 30 Mar 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) strongly condemned the Israeli occupation authorities' prevention of His Beatitude the Latin Patriarch of Al-Quds (Jerusalem), Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa, and the Custos of the Holy Land, Father Francesco Elbo, from reaching the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in occupied Al-Quds (Jerusalem) to celebrate Palm Sunday Mass
  • Spain condemns latest attacks against UNIFIL Spain MFA 30 Mar 2026 -- Spain strongly condemns the latest attacks against the UNIFIL contingent, which have resulted in the deaths of three Indonesian soldiers
  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Türkiye, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia and UAE, 30 March 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Mar 2026

  • Secretary General Alain Berset deplores serious setback following passing of law expanding use of death penalty in Israel Council of Europe 30 Mar 2026 -- The Secretary General of the Council of Europe, Alain Berset, has expressed his deep concern after the adoption, on 30 March 2026, by the Knesset, of a law expanding the use of the death penalty in Israel. This adoption comes despite repeated calls to the Israeli authorities, notably by the Council of Europe, to renounce it.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Ukraine, Somalia UNOCHA 30 Mar 2026

  • Press conference with Federal Chancellor Merz and President al-Sharaa of Syria - Support for Syria's stability and growth Germany - Federal Government 30 Mar 2026

  • Airstrike on funeral underscores rising civilian toll in Sudan UN News 30 Mar 2026 -- Fears are mounting for civilians caught up in Sudan's deadly war between rival militaries as attacks intensify and humanitarian access shrinks, following a deadly airstrike on a funeral gathering in West Kordofan.

  • Army officer killed, NPA member nabbed in Mindoro clash Philippine News Agency 30 Mar 2026 -- A Philippine Army (PA) junior officer was killed while a New People's Army (NPA) rebel was captured following an encounter in San Jose town, Occidental Mindoro on Sunday.

  • Bosnia and Herzegovina: Council extends framework for restrictive measures until March 2027 EC/CEU 30 Mar 2026 -- The Council decided today to extend the framework for restrictive measures in view of the situation in Bosnia and Herzegovina for a further twelve months, until 31 March 2027.

  • Prime Minister shares address by Shri Amit Shah in Lok Sabha on India's decisive fight against Naxalism India PIB 30 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi shared the outstanding speech delivered by Union Home Minister Shri Amit Shah ji, noting that it was filled with important facts, historical context, and a detailed account of the Government's efforts over the past decade. Shri Modi highlighted that for decades, the retrograde Maoist ideology had an adverse impact on the development of several regions, with Left Wing Extremism severely affecting the future of countless youngsters.

News Reports

  • Turkish, Syrian intel nab longtime espionage fugitive near Lebanon border Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 30 Mar 2026 -- A man accused of conducting espionage activities against Türkiye was captured on the Syria-Lebanon border on Monday in a joint operation by Turkish and Syrian intelligence services after more than a decade as a fugitive, Anadolu Agency reported, citing security sources.

  • Operation in Response to UAV incidents in South-East Finland Continues Police of Finland 30 Mar 2026 -- One of the two unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) that came down in south-east Finland on Sunday 29 March was rendered safe yesterday evening by means of a controlled explosion. The explosion was carried out because the UAV, which had come down north of Kouvola, was assessed in a preliminary evaluation by the authorities to have explosives attached.

  • Cyberthreat level remains high - attacks becoming more targeted and complex Federal Council - Swiss Government 30 Mar 2026 -- The semi-annual report published today by the National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) outlines the relevant incidents and developments relating to cyberthreats against Switzerland and internationally in the second half of 2025. Alongside voluntary reports of cyber incidents, the report presents reportable cyberattacks against critical infrastructure for the first time. Such attacks have been mandatory reporting requirements since 1 April 2025. Although the number of voluntary reports has remained stable at a high level, the nature of these incidents is evolving and becoming more targeted. Effective protection requires close cooperation between government, businesses and society at both national and international levels.

  • Meeting with Arkhangelsk Region Governor Alexander Tsybulsky President of Russia 30 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Governor of the Arkhangelsk Region Alexander Tsybulsky.
  • Telephone conversation with President of Serbia Aleksandar Vucic President of Russia 30 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of the Republic of Serbia Aleksandar Vucic at the initiative of the Serbian side.

  • Trump claims 'Cuba is next,' China's FM says it firmly supports Cuba in safeguarding sovereignty, opposes external interference Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- China consistently upholds respect for the sovereignty and territorial integrity of all countries and opposes the violation of other countries' sovereignty or interference in their internal affairs under any pretext, Mao Ning, a spokesperson for China's Foreign Ministry said on Monday when asked for China's comment regarding US President Donald Trump's claim that "Cuba is next."
  • China urges US to immediately lift its blockade, sanctions against Cuba, to aid Cuba to the best of capabilities: FM Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry that US President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island on Sunday night, adding "I told them, if a country wants to send some oil into Cuba right now, I have no problem whether it's Russia or not", and the question that whether Cuba has sought China assistance in oil and whether China is willing to help Cuba, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said that China urges the US to immediately lift its blockade and sanctions against Cuba and cease all forms of coercion and pressure.
  • Sanctioned Russian Oil Tanker Arrives In Cuban Waters After US Grants Permission RFE/RL 30 Mar 2026 -- A Russian-flagged ⁠tanker ⁠carrying Russian crude oil arrived in Cuba after Washington waived sanctions on the ship, permitting it past a de-facto US oil embargo of the Caribbean island.
  • Sanctioned Russian Oil Tanker Enters Sanctioned Cuba's Waters, Possibly With US Permission RFE/RL 30 Mar 2026 -- A sanctioned Russia-flagged ⁠tanker ⁠carrying Russian crude has entered Cuban waters, ship-tracking data show, avoiding a potentially tense showdown with the US Coast Guard and providing a lifeline to the struggling Caribbean island nation.

  • Press release on the revocation of a UK Embassy employee's accreditation Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Mar 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning's Regular Press Conference on March 30, 2026 PRC MofA 30 Mar 2026
  • China's new liquid-propellent rocket Kinetica-2 makes successful maiden flight Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- At dusk on a cool March evening in 2026, as the last rays of sunlight faded over the Gobi Desert, the Dongfeng Commercial Space Innovation Test Zone came alive with anticipation. Kinetica-2 liquid-propellant rocket, developed by China's commercial space firm CAS Space, ignited with a thunderous roar around 7 pm, roaring to life for its maiden flight.

  • U.S. Senate delegation visits Taiwan to strengthen regional alliances CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- A group of four U.S. senators, led by Democrat Jeanne Shaheen and Republican John Curtis, arrived in Taiwan early Monday for a two-day visit aimed at strengthening alliances in the Indo-Pacific region.
  • MAC urges KMT chair not to echo Beijing agenda over planned China trip CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- Taiwan's Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) on Monday urged Kuomintang (KMT) Chairperson Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) not to cater to Beijing's political agenda after she accepted an invitation from Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) to visit China next week.
  • KMT chair 'gladly accepts' Xi's invitation to visit China next week CNA 30 Mar 2026 -- Kuomintang (KMT) Chairperson Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) said Monday that she has "gladly accepted" an invitation from Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) to visit China next week, which will show that the two sides "are not destined for war."
  • KMT chair expects upcoming mainland trip to highlight peace Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- Cheng Li-wun, chairperson of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, said on Monday that her upcoming mainland trip will highlight a shared hope for peace.
  • CPC Central Committee, Xi invite KMT chairperson to visit mainland Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- The Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee and Xi Jinping, general secretary of the CPC Central Committee, have invited Cheng Li-wun, chairperson of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, to visit the Chinese mainland from April 7 to 12, a CPC official announced on Monday, according to the Xinhua News Agency.
  • China to take lawful countermeasures against Keiji Furuya for colluding with 'Taiwan independence' forces: TAO spokesperson Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- In response to a media inquiry about China's decision to take countermeasures against Keiji Furuya, a member of the House of Representatives of National Diet of Japan, Zhu Fenglian, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said that China's countermeasures against certain pro-Taiwan Japanese politicians are lawful and just actions taken in accordance with Chinese law, aimed at safeguarding national interests and consistent with international practice.
  • Chinese FM criticizes four US senators' visit to China's Taiwan region aimed at pushing for defense budget bill; US support is merely rhetorical: mainland expert Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- When asked about the visit by four US senators to the Taiwan region, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning said on Monday that China has all along opposed official contact between the US and China's Taiwan region, and has lodged serious protests with the US side.
  • China announces countermeasures against Keiji Furuya, saying Taiwan question is China's core red line: FM Global Times 30 Mar 2026 -- Regarding China's decision to impose countermeasures on Japanese House of Representatives member Keiji Furuya, Chinese FM spokesperson Mao Ning said that the Taiwan question is the core of China's core interests and an insurmountable red line. China has lodged solemn representations with the Japanese side over Furuya's visit to China's Taiwan region.
  • KMT chairperson accepts invitation to visit Chinese mainland People's Daily 30 Mar 2026 -- Cheng Li-wun, chairperson of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, on Monday expressed her thanks and accepted the invitation to visit the Chinese mainland in April.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 30 Mar 2026
  • CPC Central Committee, Xi invite KMT chairperson to visit mainland People's Daily 30 Mar 2026 -- The Communist Party of China (CPC) Central Committee and Xi Jinping, general secretary of the CPC Central Committee, have invited Cheng Li-wun, chairperson of the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party, to visit the Chinese mainland from April 7 to 12, a CPC official announced on Monday.

  • Air China resumes direct flights between Beijing and Pyongyang People's Daily 30 Mar 2026 -- Air China resumes direct flights between Beijing and Pyongyang on Monday.
  • Combating DPRK remote IT worker threats Australia DFAT 30 Mar 2026 -- On 30 March the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade, and the United States Department of State, in partnership with Global Affairs Canada, DTEX and Mandiant, convened a symposium entitled Protecting Industry from North Korean Threats in Sydney.
  • Gov't cosponsors UN resolution on human rights in North Korea KOREA.net 30 Mar 2026 -- The country will cosponsor a resolution on human rights in North Korea with the United Nations (U.N.) Human Rights Council.

  • Aboul Gheit speaks with Nechirvan Barzani after attack on residence League of Arab States 30 Mar 2026
  • Comment by Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on a drone attack targeting residence of President of the Kurdistan Region of Iraq Nechirvan Barzani Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 30 Mar 2026

  • Japan aborts maiden flight test of reusable rocket experimental vehicle again due to malfunction People's Daily 30 Mar 2026 -- Japan's space agency aborted the scheduled maiden flight test of its small reusable rocket experimental vehicle RV-X again on Monday due to a malfunction, local media reported.

  • Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for Krzyżowa Germany Federal Foreign Office 30 Mar 2026

  • World News in Brief: Ukraine drone attacks, Afghan rights, Zero-Waste Day UN News 30 Mar 2026 -- A night of drone attacks reportedly killed two people and injured 12 in the Ukrainian port city of Odesa, as a maternity hospital and three educational facilities were also damaged.
